description: External survey of CJT with correlated voters, LLM ensemble voting research, wisdom of crowds in AI, and optimal ensemble sizing
expect: External research provides quantitative CJT predictions at rho=0.62, validates regime analysis from L-564

cjt_correlated_voters.kaniovski_2010_formula: P(n,p,c) uses Bahadur representation with regularized incomplete beta function
cjt_correlated_voters.critical_example: P(3, 0.55, 0.4) = 0.5451 < 0.55 (single voter) — CJT REVERSAL at moderate correlation
cjt_correlated_voters.at_rho_0_62: damage worse than c=0.4 example — N=3 majority actively degrades accuracy when p near threshold
cjt_correlated_voters.mechanism: positive correlation causes adding jurors to degrade accuracy; negative correlation improves it

effective_sample_size.implication: going from N=3 to N=10 gains only +0.18 effective votes — diminishing returns extreme

non_monotonicity.finding: when tasks mix easy (p>0.5) and hard (p<0.5) items, majority vote accuracy is inverted-U shaped
non_monotonicity.implication: if swarm tasks include items with p<0.5, there IS a computable optimal finite N

heterogeneity_over_N.wisdom_of_silicon_crowd: 12 heterogeneous LLMs ensemble Brier=0.20, indistinguishable from 925 humans (0.19)
heterogeneity_over_N.majority_rules_content_categorization: 10-model ensemble 0.92 F1 vs best single model 0.55 F1
heterogeneity_over_N.finding: model diversity (different architectures) >> sampling diversity (same model multiple times)
heterogeneity_over_N.key: the swarm should diversify APPROACH, not multiply copies

sequential_over_parallel.finding: sequential scaling outperforms parallel self-consistency in 95.6% of configurations
sequential_over_parallel.implication: swarm's handoff-iterate pattern more valuable than simultaneous voting

better_than_majority.ISP_method: second-order aggregation 90.48% vs majority 85.13% (+5.35pp) for binary tasks
better_than_majority.OW_L_method: outperforms majority in 97.92% of 16 model combinations
better_than_majority.implication: correlation-aware aggregation provably better than naive majority vote

inference_scaling_laws.ceiling: argmax_a sum_r P(a,r) — hard ceiling determined by model distribution
inference_scaling_laws.convergence: O(c^{-n}) exponential — without oracle verifier, sampling cannot achieve perfect accuracy even with infinite samples
inference_scaling_laws.self_consistency_cost: 18.6 samples avg to match confidence-weighted methods using 10 samples (46% cost reduction possible)

diff: Expected CJT correlated-voter research to exist — confirmed via Kaniovski 2010. Did NOT expect CJT reversal to be this severe: at rho=0.62, N=3 is WORSE than N=1 for p near 0.5. N_eff formula confirms F-SP2's N_e≈15 interpretation (effective sample bottleneck). Non-monotonicity from NeurIPS 2024 is novel — explains why swarm benchmarks sometimes degrade with more agents. Sequential > parallel finding (95.6% of configs) CONFIRMS swarm handoff pattern as superior to parallel voting.
